brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is rigorous functional-analytic PDE stability: weighted energy estimates with singular weights and analytic low-rank/finite-rank corrections (from Taylor expansions in a smooth basis) that enforce exact vanishing conditions on a numerically constructed self-similar Euler profile. Paper B's machinery is statistical uncertainty quantification: bootstrap resampling (block, residual, sieve, wild) plus adaptive conformal calibration to restore coverage for dependent, non-exchangeable time series. These are unrelated processes with no role-for-role mapping; the only overlaps ('residual', 'numerical', 'correction') are keyword coincidences, not shared transferable machinery.
